skill.name: feature-design-assistant
skill.description: Guides collaborative feature planning by collecting requirements, comparing approaches, validating designs, and preparing implementation handoff details.
skill.summary: Convert feature ideas into validated technical designs and task plans.
skill.author: notedit

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these were false positives from Markdown code fences, inline backticks, and ordinary planning text. No executable scripts, network access, credential handling, prompt injection text, or cryptographic API use were found in the single SKILL.md file. The only retained risk factor is bounded filesystem guidance to write a design document under docs/designs.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Detections Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across SKILL.md lines 26-358. The reviewed locations are fenced JSON and Markdown examples plus inline task metadata, not executable code. No command execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and the flagged areas are documentation examples. I found no script file, shell invocation, or instruction that executes user-controlled commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Scanner Noise
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported weak cryptography patterns throughout SKILL.md. Review found planning prompts, question templates, and Markdown examples, but no cryptographic library calls, hash functions, encryption code, or password handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain metadata, next-step labels, and design principles. They do not contain cryptographic APIs or algorithm selections.

low_findings.title: Bounded Repository File Write Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to write a design document to docs/designs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md. This is expected behavior for a design assistant, but it can create or modify repository documentation and should remain visible to users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write target is explicitly documented and scoped to design documentation. It is a real filesystem action, but I found no destructive or hidden file operation.

content.value_statement: Feature ideas often miss requirements, constraints, and implementation tradeoffs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured discovery, design validation, and task handoff.
actual_capabilities: Collects feature goals, user types, scope, timeline, and quality requirements through structured questions.
Explores technical layers such as data models, business logic, APIs, and UI before proposing a design.
Compares two or three implementation approaches with pros, cons, and recommended fit.
Presents architecture, data model, API, component, error handling, and testing sections for review.
Creates a design document outline and implementation task checklist for handoff.
limitations: Does not implement the feature by itself.
Depends on accurate user answers and available repository context.
Does not replace security, compliance, or production architecture review.
May need adaptation for teams with strict planning templates.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Feature
use_cases.description: Turn an initial idea into requirements, design options, and an implementation-ready plan.

use_cases.title: Prepare Architecture Review Material
use_cases.description: Organize tradeoffs, dependencies, error handling, and testing strategy before a review meeting.

use_cases.title: Create Developer Handoff Tasks
use_cases.description: Convert an approved design into ordered tasks with files, priorities, dependencies, and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Feature Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use feature-design-assistant to help me design a new feature. Start by asking the core requirements questions.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Technical Scope
prompt_templates.scenario: Known product goal with unclear implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use feature-design-assistant to map the system layers, dependencies, quality requirements, and testing approach for this feature.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Implementation Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Team needs a decision between approaches
prompt_templates.prompt: Use feature-design-assistant to propose three implementation approaches, explain tradeoffs, and recommend one for our constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Handoff Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use feature-design-assistant to validate each design section, then produce a design document outline and ordered implementation tasks.

output_examples.input: Design a notifications feature for account activity.
output: A requirements summary covering users, channels, delivery rules, and reliability goals.
Several approach options with tradeoffs for synchronous, asynchronous, and hybrid delivery.
A reviewed design outline with data model, API, error handling, and testing sections.

output_examples.input: Prepare tasks for a completed feature design.
output: A concise implementation checklist grouped by model, API, UI, and testing work.
Each task includes priority, likely files, dependencies, and verification steps.
A final handoff choice for immediate execution, a new session, later work, or design revision.

best_practices: Provide product goals, affected users, constraints, and known dependencies before asking for design options.
Validate each design section before moving to implementation tasks.
Review generated documents and task lists before committing them to the repository.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to skip stakeholder clarification for unclear requirements.
Treating suggested architecture as final without repository review.
Generating implementation tasks before selecting and validating an approach.

faq.question: Does this skill write production code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on requirements, design options, documentation, and implementation planning.

faq.question: Can it work with an existing codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. It directs the assistant to inspect project structure, patterns, related modules, and recent changes.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of required network services was found in the skill file.

faq.question: Why is the risk level low?
faq.answer: Static command and cryptography alerts were false positives, but the skill can guide creation of a design document.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it before significant feature work, architecture changes, or implementation handoffs that need clear decisions.
